records.ety: [L. petra rock, Gr. pe`tra (akin to pe`tros a stone) + -fy: cf. F. ptrifier. Cf. Parrot, Petrel, Pier.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Petrified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Petrifying (?).]

defs: To convert, as any animal or vegetable matter, into stone or stony substance; as, petrified wood.
quotes: A river that petrifies any sort of wood or leaves.

defs: To make callous or obdurate; to transform, as by petrifaction; as, to petrify the heart. Young.
quotes: And petrify a genius to a dunce.
A hideous fatalism, which ought, logically, to petrify your volition.

defs: To paralyze, especially with fear; to stupefy; as, she was petrified by the sight of the bear in her tent.
quotes: The poor, petrified journeyman, quite unconscious of what he was doing.

defs: To become stone, or of a stony hardness, as organic matter by calcareous deposits.
Fig.: To become stony, callous, or obdurate.
quotes: Like Niobe we marble grow, And petrify with grief.
